content.problemStatement:
The greatest common divisor (GCD) of a sequence is the greatest positive integer which divides each element of this sequence.

You are given a sequence AAA of positive integers with size NNN. You are allowed to delete up to N−1N-1N−1 elements from this sequence. (I.e., you may delete any number of elements, including zero, as long as the resulting sequence is non-empty.)

Please find the minimum number of elements which have to be deleted so that the GCD of the resulting sequence would be equal to 111, or determine that it is impossible.
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains a single integer N. The second line contains N space-separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the minimum number of elements to delete, or −1 if it is impossible to make the GCD equal to 1.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Example case 1: The GCD is already equal to 1, so the answer is 0.
Example case 2: It is impossible to make the GCD equal to 1, so the answer is -1.
The GCD of the sequence is 2, and it is impossible to make the GCD equal to 1 by deleting elements.
The GCD of the sequence is already equal to 1, so the answer is 0.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we need to find the minimum number of elements to delete from the given sequence so that the GCD of the resulting sequence is equal to 1. We can use the concept of GCD and the properties of prime numbers to solve this problem. We will iterate over all possible subsets of the given sequence and calculate the GCD of each subset. If the GCD of a subset is equal to 1, we will update our answer with the minimum number of elements to delete.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N * 2^N) — where T is the number of test cases, N is the number of elements in the sequence, and 2^N is the number of possible subsets of the sequence.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — where N is the number of elements in the sequence.
